codeReview.summary: Mission dir IPC/watch plumbing, typed disk payload parsing, and late-file handling are implemented, but the documented restart fallback contract is incomplete because fallback resolution uses the current session id instead of the required base session id.

issues.description: `resolveMissionDirPath` falls back to `~/.factory/missions/<sessionId>`, but the Mission spec requires `~/.factory/missions/<baseSessionId>`. When a restored or replaced session id differs from the base mission id, restart recovery can watch and read the wrong directory, so VAL-RECOVERY-004 is not fully satisfied.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `mission-core-worker` skill requires writing/running failing tests before implementation, but this worker edited code before the first targeted failing-state test execution while still reporting `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The transcript skeleton for worker session `6c1209b9-e713-49d4-8cae-b332ac00a33c` shows multiple `ApplyPatch` calls before the first Execute step described as observing the current failing state, while the handoff file `handoffs/2026-03-09T04-16-06-559Z__mission-dir-watcher-ipc-and-path-capture__6c1209b9-e713-49d4-8cae-b332ac00a33c.json` records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Review failed: the feature mostly delivers the Electron-only missionDir reader/watch bridge and persistence wiring, but its restart fallback does not implement the required base-session missionDir convention, so recovery can target the wrong directory after session id replacement.
